Literally: may your breath be warm. The warm, casual thank-you between friends. Dameshun garm = good on them.
The cloth spread on floor or table where the meal lives. Sar-e sofre biā = come to the table. Sofre-ye aghd: the wedding spread.

fields.back: to stop speaking to someone (in a huff)
fields.extra: The named institution of Iranian relationships: bāham ghahran = they are not on speaking terms. The reconciliation is āshti: āshti kardan = to make up.
The Iranian week starts here: shanbe is day one, and the weekend is Thursday afternoon and Friday.
Yek (one) + shanbe: one-past-Saturday. The days simply count up from shanbe; you already know them all.
Do (two) + shanbe. Doshanbe mibinamet = see you Monday. Also the capital of Tajikistan, named for its Monday market.
Se (three) + shanbe. Se-shanbe ba'd az zohr = Tuesday afternoon.
Chahār (four) + shanbe. Chahārshanbe-suri: the fire-jumping festival on the year's last Wednesday eve.
Panj (five) + shanbe. The half-day that opens the weekend; family visits and trips to shomāl begin.
The holy day and day off, Iran's Sunday. Jom'e-hā ta'tile = Fridays everything is closed. Ghorub-e jom'e = Friday dusk, famously melancholic.
Sā'at-e do o nim = half past two (two and a half). Nim sā'at = half an hour. Nesf halves things; nim builds times and measures.
Ye rob' dige miram = I leave in a quarter hour. Sā'at-e se o rob' = quarter past three.
Akhiran didamesh = I saw him recently. From akhir, latest: akhbār-e akhir = the latest news.
Kār-e fori dāram = I have something urgent. Fori biā = come at once. Fowran = immediately (formal).
The written word; speech says yeho: yeho ghāyeb shod = he suddenly vanished. Nāgahāni = unexpected, abrupt.
Fe'lan injām = for now I am here. And the breezy goodbye: fe'lan! = later!
Emruz ta'tile = today is a holiday / it is closed. Ta'tilāt = the holidays. Ta'til-e rasmi = official holiday.
Parvāz ta'khir dāre = the flight is delayed. Bā ta'khir residam = I arrived late (with delay).
Ghorub-e āftāb = sundown. Moghe'-e ghorub = at dusk. Its dawn twin: tolu'.
Hafte-ye āyande = next week. Dar āyande = in the future. From āmadan: that which is coming.
Hafte-ye gozashte = last week. Dar gozashte = in the past. From gozashtan: that which has passed.
Glued on the end: ketābam = my book, delam = my heart, midunam = I know. One tiny letter does the work of my.
Ketābet = your book, dustet dāram = I love you (love-you-I). Formal version: -etān (ketābetun = your book, polite).

fields.back: his, her, its; him, her, it (attached)
fields.extra: Ketābesh = his book. Didamesh = I saw him. Esmesh chiye? = what is its name? The busiest little syllable in spoken Persian.
Spoken: -emun, -etun, -eshun. Khunamun = our house, māshinetun = your (pl.) car, pedareshun = their father.
The bazaar unit: do kilu portoghāl = two kilos of oranges. Kiluyi chand? = how much per kilo?
Sad metr jelotar = a hundred meters ahead. Chand metr? = how many meters? Metr be metr = meter by meter.
Bist daraje = twenty degrees. Daraje-ye harārat = temperature. Daraje-dār = ranking officer.
Dar (in) + sad (hundred): in a hundred. Panjāh darsad takhfif = fifty percent off. Sad darsad! = one hundred percent, absolutely!
The bazaar's magic word: takhfif nemidid? = will you not give a discount? Always asked, often granted, part of the dance.
Kharj kardan = to spend. Kharjesh ziāde = it costs a lot to run. Kam-kharj = economical.
Vazn kam kardam = I lost weight. Vaznet chande? = how much do you weigh? Also the meter of poetry.
Vasāyelam = my things, my stuff. Be vasile-ye = by means of. Vasile-ye naghliye = a vehicle (means of transport).
Asbāb (gear) + bāzi (play): play-gear. Maghāze-ye asbāb-bāzi = toy store.
Ye ja'be shirini = a box of pastries, the correct thing to arrive holding. Ja'be-ye abzār = toolbox.
Kise mikhāyn? = do you want a bag? (checkout question). Kise-ye zobāle = trash bag. Also the scrubbing mitt of the old hammām.
Bargh raft = the power went out. Bargh-e cheshmāsh = the gleam in her eyes. Barghkār = electrician.
Ojāgh-e gāz = gas stove. Gāz gereftan = to bite. Gāzesh bezan = take a bite of it.
Internet dāri? = do you have internet? Internetam tamum shod = my data ran out. Vāy-fāy = wifi.
Poshte kāmpiuteram = I am at my computer. The formal Persian coinage rāyāne exists; everyone says kāmpiuter.
Nur-e āftāb = sunlight. Nur kam-e = the light is low. A beloved name and half of many mosque names.
Tu sāye beshin = sit in the shade. Zir-e sāye-ye shomā = under your protection (a graceful thanks).
The walled heart of the traditional Persian house: a pool (howz), geraniums, grandmother's rug. Tu hayāt bāzi konid = play in the yard.
Az pelle-hā boro bālā = take the stairs up. Pelle pelle = step by step. Rāh-pelle = stairwell.
Dush gereftan = to take a shower (you take it, like a taxi). Also shoulder: bār bar dush = a burden on one's shoulders.
Ghofl kardan = to lock; also to freeze up (phones and brains alike: gushim ghofl kard). Ghofl o kelid = lock and key.
Dur (far) + bin (seer): the far-seer, originally binoculars. Durbin-e gushi = phone camera. Labkhand bezan be durbin = smile for the camera.
Dastāto bā sābun beshur = wash your hands with soap. Sābun be delesh zad = he got his hopes up (soaped his heart).
Spoken: āyne. Tu āyine negāh kard = she looked in the mirror. Mirrors flank the wedding sofre and the haft-sin: reflection, light, truth.
Chatr bebar, bārun miād = take an umbrella, it is going to rain. Chatr-e nejāt = parachute (rescue umbrella).
Āshghāl-hā ro bordam birun = I took out the trash. Also the insult: āshghāl! = you piece of garbage!
Posht-e bām = the rooftop, where summer nights were slept away under the stars. Bām-e tehrān = the mountain lookout over the city.
Zir-e patu = under the blanket, the correct winter address. Patu ro bekesh ru khodet = pull the blanket over you.
The Persian garden (bāgh-e irāni) is a UNESCO art form; paradise itself was a Persian walled bāgh. Bāgh-e anār = pomegranate orchard.
Garmā-ye tābestun = the summer heat. Garm (warm) + -ā makes the noun, exactly like sarmā from sard.
Remember the wall proverb: divār mush dāre, mush ham gush dāre. Mush-e kāmpiuter = computer mouse, borrowed meaning and all.
Barg-e derakht = tree leaf. Barg-rizān = autumn leaf-fall. And on the grill: kabāb-e barg, meat sliced leaf-thin.
Rishe-ye kalame = a word's root, your best tool in this deck. Rishe davāndan = to take root. Az rishe = from the root, fundamentally.
Otāgh bā manzare = a room with a view. Manzare-ye kuh = the mountain view. From nazar, looking.
Wild tulips are Iran's spring flame; the flower and the name are Persian long before Holland. Lālezār = tulip field, once Tehran's theater street.
Tufān miād = a storm is coming. Tufāni = stormy, also a person on a rampage. English typhoon is a cousin.
The workhorse of proverbs and insults: khar nasho = do not be a fool. Az khar sho pāyin = get off your high horse (donkey).
Proverb: shotor didi? nadidi = you saw a camel? you saw nothing (keep what you saw to yourself). Shotor-morgh = ostrich, the camel-bird.
Berim shomāl! = let us go north! In Tehran, shomāl means the green Caspian shore, the national weekend escape.
Jonub-e shahr = the south of the city. Jonubi = southern(er). The deep south means the Persian Gulf coast.
Shargh-e irān = eastern Iran. Khāvar-miāne = Middle East (khāvar is the older word for east).
Gharbi = western; farhang-e gharbi = Western culture. Gharb-e tehrān = west Tehran.
The national obsession. Derby-e tehrān: Perspolis (red) versus Esteghlāl (blue) splits every family.
Tup bāzi = ball games. Originally the cannon; the leap from cannonball to football took one century. Tup-e = it is top-notch (slang).
Bāzi kardan = to play. Bāzi-ye komputeri = video game. Bāzigush = playful (play-eared). Jer nazan, bāzi kon = no cheating, play on.
Tim-e melli = the national team, Team Melli, follow it and you have small talk forever. Tim-e mā bord = our team won.
Mosābeghe-ye futbāl = football match. Mosābeghe bedim? = shall we race? Mosābeghāt = tournaments.
Jāyeze bordan = to win a prize. Jāyeze-ye nobel = the Nobel Prize. Barande-ye jāyeze = prize winner.
From bordan (to win): the taker. Barande shod = he won. Its rhyming twin bāzande = loser (from bākhtan, to lose).
Miram bāshgāh = I am going to the gym. Bāshgāh-e futbāl = football club.
Sāhel-e daryā = the seashore. Kenār-e sāhel ghadam zadim = we walked along the beach.
Say it correctly and win every Iranian heart: khalij-e fārs = the Persian Gulf. Khalij-e fārs tā abad = forever the Persian Gulf.
Ham (same) + kār (work): work-sharer. Hamkāram = my colleague. Hamkāri = cooperation.
Fāmilāmun = our relatives, the whole clan of aunts and cousins. Esm o fāmil = first and last name (also the beloved paper game).
The polite I: bande fekr mikonam ... = your servant thinks ... Literally: slave, servant (of God). Everyday humility built into grammar.
Ghiāfash bānamake = he has a cute face. Ghiāfe nagir = do not make that face, drop the attitude.
Lahje dāram? = do I have an accent? Lahje-ye esfahāni = the Esfahani accent, Iran's most musical. Bi-lahje = accent-free.
Ghiāfat āshnāst = you look familiar. Dust o āshnā = friends and acquaintances, one's circle.
Gharibe nistim! = we are no strangers (drop the formality). Bā gharibe-hā harf nazan = do not talk to strangers.
From khārej, outside. Zabān-e khāreji = foreign language. As a learner in Iran you are the beloved khāreji who tries.
Spoken: ishun. The polite third person: ishun modir-e mā hastan = he (respected) is our manager, verb in plural. Respect is grammar in Persian.
Casual and slightly dismissive: yāru migoft ... = so this guy was saying ... Never to someone's face.
Bale ghorbān = yes sir (military and butlers). Root of the everyday endearment ghorbunet beram = may I be sacrificed for you = thanks, dear.
Sennet chande? = how old are you? (asked freely and early; do not be startled). Ham-senn = the same age.
Ghadesh bolande = he is tall. Ghad keshidi! = you have shot up! Ghadd-o-bālā = stature, figure.
The written kheyli: besyār khub = very well. You will read it everywhere and say kheyli instead.
Tamām-e ruz = all day. Tamām-e sa'yam ro kardam = I gave it everything. Tamum! = done, finished!
Az in samt = this way. Be samt-e tehrān = toward Tehran. Samt-e chap = the left side.
Gushe-ye khiābun = the street corner. Ye gushe vāisā = stand off to the side. Gushe-gir = withdrawn, a corner-keeper.

fields.back: after, in pursuit of; looking for
fields.extra: Donbāl-e chi migardi? = what are you looking for? Donbālam biā = follow me. Donbāl-e kār = job-hunting.
Dar mowred-e chi? = about what? Dar in mowred = on this matter. Mowred-e alāghe-ye man = my favorite (case of my liking).
Manzuram in nabud = that is not what I meant. Manzuret chiye? = what are you getting at? Key phrase for repairs in conversation.
Che jāleb! = how interesting! Jālebe ke ... = the interesting thing is that ... Barām jālebe = I find it intriguing.
Vaz'iat chetore? = how are things? Vaz'iat-e ghermez = red alert (dire straits). Vaz' also works alone: vaz'am khube.

fields.back: flow; what's going on, the story
fields.extra: Jarayān chiye? = what is going on here? Dar jarayān bāsh = stay in the loop (in the flow). Jarayān-e āb = the water current.
Makhsusan to = especially you. Makhsus = special, reserved: pitzā-ye makhsus = the house special pizza.

fields.back: just like this; for no reason, casually
fields.extra: Cherā umadi? hamintori = why did you come? no reason, just because. Hamintori ke hast khube = it is fine as it is.
Ettefāghan man ham unjā budam = as it happens, I was there too. Corrects assumptions gracefully: ettefāghan bar'aks-e = actually it is the opposite.
The subject-changer: rāsti, shām chi mikhorim? = by the way, what is for dinner? From rāst, truth: now that I think of it truly.
Zud biā, vagarna dir mishe = come quickly, otherwise it will get late. Va (and) + agar (if) + na (not).
The written also; essays and news love it. In reply to good wishes: hamchenin! = likewise, same to you!
Yeki az dustām = one of my friends. Yeki bud yeki nabud = there was one, there was no one: the Persian once upon a time.
Vāy khodā = oh my God. Vāy che khoshgele! = wow, how pretty! Ey vāy = oh dear, spilled-tea energy.
The sound of delight at a table or a view: bah-bah, che bui! = ah, what an aroma! Its rival nach-nach is disapproval.
Āfarin be to! = good for you! The teacher's gold star word. Stronger: bārikallā!
Heyf shod = what a shame (it became a pity). Heyf-e un ghazā = that food deserved better. Che heyf = alas.
The protest word: ākhe cherā? = but why? Ākhe man az kojā bedunam? = come on, how would I know? Pure spoken Persian.
Spoken: inghad. Inghadr khubi? = how are you this good? Inghad harf nazan = do not talk so much.
Kāsh budi = I wish you were here (half of Persian poetry in two words). Ey kāsh midunestam = if only I knew.
Ehtemālan miām = I will probably come. From ehtemāl, probability: ehtemālesh kame = odds are low.
Do'ā mikonam barāt = I will pray for you, I am rooting for you. Do'ā-ye mādar = a mother's prayer, the strongest force known.
Spoken: ishālā. Attached to every future plan: fardā miām, inshāallāh. Sometimes a promise, sometimes a soft maybe; context tells.
Said over children, appetites, and successes to admire without jinxing: māshāllā che bozorg shode! = my, how he has grown!
Religiously permitted, and figuratively clean-earned: pul-e halāl = honest money. Halālet bāshe = you have earned it, enjoy it.
The opposite of halāl. Also everyday hyperbole: hayf o harām shod = it went to waste. Harām nakon = do not waste it.
Eaten like fruit, peeled, salted, passed around the living room. Māst o khiār = the yogurt-cucumber side everyone fights over.
Āb-havij = carrot juice, served with bastani on top in juice bars. Havij bastani: try it before doubting it.
Māh-e asal = honeymoon (honey month). Asalam = my honey. Cheshm-hā-ye asali = honey-colored eyes.
Spoken: bādemjun. The soul of kashk-e bādemjun and mirzā ghāsemi. Proverb: bādemjun-e bam āfat nadāre = the tough ones survive everything.
Spoken: khoresht. The daily architecture of Persian food: khoresh-e ghorme sabzi, gheyme, fesenjān, ladled over polo.
Chāy-e shirin = sweet tea. Zabān-e shirin = a sweet tongue, charming talk. Shirin is also the legendary beloved of Persian romance.
Ghahve-ye talkh = bitter coffee. Haghighat-e talkh = the bitter truth. Khande-ye talkh = a bitter smile.
Ghazā shur shode = the food came out salty (the cook is in love, says the proverb). Also fervor: shur o showgh = passion and zeal.
Anār-e torsh = sour pomegranate. Torshi = the pickles on every table. Ghiāfe-ye torsh = a sour face.
Rosewater, sour cherry, or lime-basil seed sharbat over ice: summer hospitality in a glass. English sherbet and sorbet are its children.
From nushidan, to drink. Nushābe-ye meshki = cola (black soda). Ordered beside every kabāb by doctor's despair.
The word that fills Hafez and Khayyām, where the cup stands for love, freedom, and everything forbidden. Vocabulary of poetry above all.
Spoken: hendevune. Summer's fruit and Yaldā night's centerpiece. Hendevune zir-e baghal gozāshtan = to flatter someone (tuck a watermelon under their arm).
Angur-e shirin = sweet grapes. Dried it becomes keshmesh (raisin); juiced and aged it becomes poetry (see sharāb).
Spoken: dune. Ye dune sib = one apple. Dune dune = one by one. Ye dune-i = you are one of a kind.
Ye joft kafsh = a pair of shoes. Joft o tāgh = even and odd. Joftesh kon = park it neatly (make it a pair).
Baghiye kojān? = where are the others? Baghiyash bemune barāye fardā = save the rest for tomorrow. Baghiye-ye pul = the change.
The enthusiastic thumbs-up: āliye! = it is perfect! Chetori? āli! = how are you? Great!
Film-e mozakhraf = a terrible movie. Mozakhraf nagu = do not talk nonsense.
Tanbal nasho = do not be lazy. Tanbali kardan = to laze about. Tanbal-khāne = a den of idlers.
Bachche-ye zerang = a sharp kid. Zerangi nakon = do not try to be slick. Practical cunning, admired and watched.
Vahshat (terror) + -nāk. Terāfik vahshatnāk bud = the traffic was horrific. Also an amplifier: vahshatnāk khaste-am = I am terribly tired.
Ghazā-ye sālem = healthy food. Sālem resid = it arrived in one piece. Sālem bāshi = stay healthy (reply to a sneeze-adjacent world).
Zende bād = long live ...! Pakhsh-e zende = live broadcast. Zende bemun = stay alive, hang in there.
Movaffagh bāshi = good luck, be successful (the standard send-off before exams). Movaffaghiat = success.
The congratulation engine: tavallodet mobārak = happy birthday, eydet mobārak = happy new year, khune-ye now mobārak = congrats on the new home.
Moft-e = it is free. Mofti gir āvordam = I got it for nothing. Moft-khor = freeloader.
Kāfiye! = that is enough! (both portions and arguments). Be andāze-ye kāfi = sufficiently.
Basse! = enough, stop it! Bas kon = cut it out. Az bas ke khubi = because you are just that good.
Hāzeri? = ready? Hāzeram = I am ready. Also roll call: hāzer! = present! Hāzer-javāb = quick-witted (ready-answered).
Ghazā āmāde-ast = the food is ready. Āmāde bāsh = be prepared. Hāzer is the spoken snap; āmāde the standard word.
Esm-e kāmel = full name. Kāmelan = completely: kāmelan dorost-e = it is completely right.
Cherāgh khāmush-e = the light is off. Gushim khāmush bud = my phone was off. Khāmush kon = turn it off.
Mashghulam = I am busy. Mashghul-e kār = at work, mid-task. Khat mashghul-e = the line is busy.
Bi (without) + kār (work). Emruz bikāram = I am free today. Bikār nashin = do not sit idle. Bikāri = unemployment.
Che nāze! = how cute! Nāz kardan = to play coy, make yourself begged; nāzesh ro bekesh = indulge her sulking. A whole art form.
Bā (with) + maze (taste): tasty-personed. Kheyli bāmazei = you are hilarious. Said of jokes, kids, and cats.
Khosh + tip (from French type): of pleasant style. Che khoshtip shodi! = look how sharp you are! (say it, it lands).
Ma'lume = obviously (it is evident). Ma'lum nist = it is unclear, who knows. Az kojā ma'lum? = says who, how do we know?
Do barābar = double (two equals). Dar barābar-e = in the face of, against. Barābari = equality.
Movāzeb bāsh! = be careful! (traffic, steps, hearts). Movāzeb-e khodet bāsh = take care of yourself, the standard goodbye add-on.
Motma'eni? = are you sure? Motma'en bāsh = rest assured. Motma'ennan = certainly.
Dāstān-e vāghe'i = a true story. Dust-e vāghe'i = a real friend. From vāghe'iat, reality.
So'āl-e shakhsi = a personal question (asked anyway, with love). Māshin-e shakhsi = private car.
Harf-e khosusi = a private word. Madrese-ye khosusi = private school. Opposite: omumi.
Dastshuyi-ye omumi = public restroom. Etelā'āt-e omumi = general knowledge, trivia. Vasile-ye naghliye-ye omumi = public transport.
Gij shodam = I am confused / my head is spinning. Gijam nakon = do not muddle me. Gij o mang = dazed and confused.
Del (heart) + tang (tight): tight-hearted. Deltangetam = I miss you. Deltangi = longing, the mood of half of Persian music.
Akhlāghesh khube = he is good-natured. Bad-akhlāgh = grumpy. Akhlāghi = ethical.
Ādat kardam = I have gotten used to it. Ādat mishe = you will get used to it (the answer to every complaint). Tark-e ādat = breaking a habit.
Alāghe dāram be ... = I am interested in ... Mowred-e alāghe = favorite. Bā alāghe = with enthusiasm.
Khosh-salighe = tasteful, stylish (high praise for hosts and gift-givers). Salighe-i-e = it is a matter of taste.
Raftāresh ajib bud = his behavior was odd. Khosh-raftār = well-behaved. From raftan: how one goes about.
Shak dāram = I have doubts. Bedun-e shak = without a doubt. Behesh shak dāram = I suspect him.
Hads bezan! = guess! Hadsam dorost bud = my guess was right. Ghābel-e hads = predictable.
Chāre-i nist = there is no other way. Chāre chiye? = what can be done? Bichāre = the poor thing (one without remedy).
Bahs nakon = do not argue. Bahs-e siāsi = political debate, the national indoor sport. Mowred-e bahs = under discussion.
Jalase dāram = I am in a meeting (the universal excuse). Jalase-ye kāri = work meeting.
Gozāresh bede = report in. Gozāresh-e khabari = news report. Gozāreshgar = reporter, commentator.
Parvande-ye pezeshki = medical file. Parvandash sangine = he has a heavy record. Parvande baste shod = case closed.
Madrak-e dāneshgāhi = university degree, the family holy grail. Bedun-e madrak = without proof. Madārek = one's papers.
Emzā konid lotfan = sign here please. Emzāsh ro gereftam = I got his autograph. Emzā kardan = to sign.
Naghshe-ye tehrān = a map of Tehran. Naghshe keshidan = to scheme, hatch a plan. Naghshe chiye? = what is the plan?
Emkānesh hast? = is it possible? Emkān nadāre! = no way, impossible! Emkānāt = amenities, resources.
Plural of shart. Tu in sharāyet = under these circumstances. Sharāyetam khub nist = I am not in a good place right now.
Asar nakard = it had no effect. Asar-e honari = artwork. Dar asar-e = as a result of. Āsār-e bāstāni = ancient monuments.
Rābete-ye mā khube = our relationship is good. Dar rābete bā = in relation to, regarding. Ravābet-e omumi = public relations.
Tamās begir = get in touch, call. Dar tamās bāshim = let us stay in touch. Tamās-e az dast rafte = missed call.
Lāzeme = it is necessary. Lāzem nist = no need. Har chi lāzem dāri begu = tell me whatever you need.
Majburam beram = I have to go. Majbur nisti = you do not have to. Majburam kardan = they made me.
Be zur = by force; barely: be zur residam = I barely made it. Zur nagu = do not strong-arm me (do not talk force). Zurash nemirese = he has not the strength.
Ghodrat-e mardom = the power of the people. Por-ghodrat = powerful. Ghodratesh ro nadāram = I do not have it in me.
Niru-ye entezāmi = the police force. Niruhā = forces, staff. Niruy-e jāzebe = gravity (the pulling force).
Taghsir-e man nist = it is not my fault. Taghsir-e ki bud? = whose fault was it? Bi-taghsir = blameless.
Tavajjoh konid = attention please (every station announcement). Jalb-e tavajjoh = attracting notice. Bi-tavajjohi = neglect.
Ehsās mikonam ke ... = I feel that ... Ehsāsāti = emotional, sentimental. Hess is the gut sense; ehsās the fuller feeling.
Talāsh kardan = to strive. Talāshet ro bekon = give it your best. Bā talāsh-e ziād = with great effort.
Khat-e otobus = bus route. Khattet khoshgele = your handwriting is lovely. Khat-khati = scribbled. Khat-e ghermez = red line.
Noghte-ye za'f = weak point. Noghte be noghte = point by point. The dots also distinguish half the alphabet; respect the noghte.
Safhe-ye panj = page five. Safhe-ye gushi = phone screen. Safhe-kelid = keyboard (key-page).
Che bu-ye khubi! = what a good smell! Bu-ye ghazā miād = I smell food. Buesh kon = take a sniff of it. Khosh-bu = fragrant.
Maze-ye khune = the taste of home. Maze kon = have a taste. Bā-maze = tasty or funny; bi-maze = bland or unfunny. One scale for food and jokes.
Futbāl tamāshā mikonim = we watch the game. Tamāshāchi = spectator. Tamāshāyi = worth watching, a sight.
Tasavvor kon = imagine (the Persian cover of the John Lennon idea). Tasavvoresh ro bokon = just picture it. Ghābel-e tasavvor nist = unimaginable.
Tarjome kon barām = translate for me. Tarjome-ye fārsi = the Persian translation. Motarjem = translator.
The learner's plea: mishe tekrār konid? = could you repeat that? Tekrār mādar-e yādgiri-st = repetition is the mother of learning; this deck agrees.
Har ruz tamrin kon = practice every day. Tamrin-e fārsi = Persian practice. Bāyad bishtar tamrin konam = I must practice more.
Az hefz = by heart: she'r ro az hefzam = I know the poem by heart. Iranians hefz whole divans of Hafez; you are in good company.
Deghghat kon = look carefully, mind the details. Bā deghghat = carefully, precisely. Bi-deghghati = carelessness.
Literally: to come into the world. Kojā be donyā umadi? = where were you born? Bachche be donyā umad = the baby arrived.

fields.back: to let go, to drop, to dump
fields.extra: Velesh kon = let it go, forget it (the daily-use serenity phrase). Velam kon = leave me alone. Vel nakon = do not let go.

fields.back: to pass (by); to fail; to cross
fields.extra: Az khiābun rad sho = cross the street. Az jolo-ye khunatun rad shodam = I passed by your place. Tu emtehān rad shodam = I failed the exam.
Tu terāfik gir kardam = I am stuck in traffic. Dargir nasho = do not get entangled. Gir dādan = to nag, to fixate: behem gir nade = get off my case.

fields.back: to offer politely; to stand on ceremony
fields.extra: The verb of the ritual: ta'ārof nakon! = do not be polite, help yourself, the sentence that unlocks every Iranian table.
Stem: nush. Everyday speech says khordan, but nush lives in the toasts: nush! = cheers! Nush-e jān = may it nourish your soul.
Hol nade! = do not push! (metro survival phrase). Māshin ro hol dādim = we pushed the car.
Savār sho = get in, hop on. Savār-e otobus shodam = I boarded the bus. Asb-savāri = horseriding.

fields.back: to get off, get out (of a vehicle)
fields.extra: Injā piāde misham = I will get off here (the taxi sentence). Piāde beram? = should I walk (go on foot)?
Mi-arze = it is worth it. Nemi-arze = not worth it. Be zahmatesh mi-arze = worth the trouble. Arzesh = value.
Namak ezāfe kon = add salt. Chizi ezāfe konam? = shall I add anything? Ezāfe = extra: vazn-e ezāfe = excess weight.

fields.back: to take off; to earn; to pull out
fields.extra: Kafshāto dar biār = take your shoes off (you will say this daily). Pul dar miāre = he earns money. Adāsh ro dar nayār = do not mimic him.
Khosh gozasht? = did you have fun? Kheyli khosh gozasht = it was wonderful. Behet khosh begzare = have a great time.
Farār kon! = run! Az dast-e kār farār mikonam = I am escaping work. Farāri = fugitive; also the nickname of every Ferrari.
Nejātam dādi = you saved me. Nejāt gharigh = lifeguard (rescuer of the drowning). Nejāt peydā kardan = to survive, be saved.
Hamle kardand = they attacked. Hamle-ye ghalbi = heart attack. Zir-e hamle = under attack.
Shelik nakon = hold your fire. Shelik-e khande = a burst of laughter, the only shooting this deck endorses.
Mobāreze bā bimāri = the fight against illness. Mobārez = fighter, campaigner. Mobāreze-ye āzādi = freedom struggle.
Az khodet mohāfezat kon = protect yourself. Mohāfez = bodyguard. Mohāfeze-kār = conservative (protection-inclined).
Parvāz-e sā'at-e hasht = the eight o'clock flight. Parvāz kardan = to fly. Parvāzam ta'khir dāre = my flight is delayed. From par, wing: flight is what wings do.
Ghatār key harekat mikone? = when does the train leave? Harekat kon = get moving. Bi-harekat = motionless.
The sign on doors: vorud mamnu' = no entry. Vorudi = entrance, admission fee. Be irān khosh āmadid: your vorud will be celebrated.
The other sign: khoruj = exit. Dar-e khoruj = exit door. Khoruji = the way out, output.
Payām bede = text me. Payāmet resid = got your message. Payām-e sowti = voice message. Payāmbar = messenger, prophet.
Shabake-ye se = Channel 3 (football lives there). Shabake-hā-ye ejtemā'i = social networks. Az kodum shabake? = on which channel?
Hāfezam za'if shode = my memory has gotten weak. Hāfeze-ye gushi = phone storage. Hāfez, the poet, means the one who knows the Koran by heart.
Talaffozam khube? = is my pronunciation good? Chetor talaffoz mishe? = how is it pronounced? Your best question in this whole deck.
Saf-e nun = the bread line at the bakery, a social institution. Tu saf vāisā = wait in line. Saf keshidan = to queue up.
Nowbat-e man-e = it is my turn. Nowbat gereftam = I booked an appointment. Be nowbat = in turns, orderly.
Ozr mikhām = I apologize, pardon me (a notch above bebakhshid). Ozr-khāhi kardan = to apologize. Ozr-e badtar az gonāh = an excuse worse than the sin.
Tabrik migam! = congratulations! Tabrik be shomā = congrats to you. Kārt-e tabrik = greeting card.
Movāfegham = I agree. Movāfeghi? = do you agree? Movāfeghat kard = he consented.
Mokhālefam = I disagree. Jahat-e mokhālef = the opposite direction. Mokhālefat kardan = to oppose.
The ta'ārof workhorse: zahmat nakesh = do not trouble yourself. Bi-zahmat = if it is no trouble (then a request follows). Zahmat keshidi = you went to such trouble, thank you.
Lotf dāri = you are too kind. Ye lotfi bekon = do me a favor. Lotfan (please) is this word wearing its adverb hat.
Dar khedmatam = at your service, I am all yours. Khedmat-e shomā = (I bring this) to you, said while handing anything over. Sarbāzi is khedmat too: military service.
Be khune-ye mā khosh āmadid = welcome to our home. Spoken: khosh umadi! The reply to a guest's arrival, followed immediately by tea.
Jorm nist ke = it is not a crime, is it. Sahne-ye jorm = crime scene. Mojrem = criminal, the guilty one.
Zendāni = prisoner. Oftād zendān = he landed in prison. Zendān-e evin: Tehran's infamous one.
Dād (justice) + gāh (place): the place of justice. Miram dādgāh = I am due in court. Dādgostari = the justice ministry.
Aslahe dāre! = he is armed! Aslahe-ye sard = a cold weapon (knife). Khal'-e selāh = disarmament.
Tasādof kardam = I had an accident (Tehran traffic's favorite sentence). Bar hasb-e tasādof = by sheer chance.
Hasudim mishe = I get jealous. Hasud = a jealous person. Hasudi nakon = do not be envious.
Ashkam dar umad = it brought me to tears. Ashk-e showgh = tears of joy. Ashk rikhtan = to shed tears: ashk rikht = she wept.
Kamaram dard mikone = my back hurts (the national ailment). Kamar-band = belt (waist-binder). Kamar bastan = to gird oneself, commit.
From khāstan, to want: the formal evening when a suitor's family comes for tea and asks. Umadan khāstegārish = they came to ask for her hand.
Ghesse begu = tell a story (the bedtime plea). Ghesse-ye mā be sar resid: our tale is done, the closing line of Persian storytelling.
Bāzi (play) + -gar (doer): the player. Bāzigar-e ma'ruf = a famous actor. Bāzigari = acting.
From khāndan, which is both to sing and to read: one word, both artists. Khānande-ye mahbubam = my favorite singer.
Kār (work) + gardān (turner): the one who runs the show. Kārgardānhā-ye irāni collect Oscars and Palmes; drop Farhadi's name and watch faces light up.
Sham' roshan kardan = to light a candle. Sham'hā ro fut kon = blow out the candles. Sham' o gol o parvāne: candle, rose, moth, the love triangle of Persian poetry.
Jazire-ye kish o gheshm = Kish and Qeshm, the Persian Gulf getaways. Jazire-ye tanhāyi = an island of loneliness.
The villain of every shepherd tale. Gorg-e bārun dide = a weathered wolf: someone too experienced to fool. Gorg o mish = twilight (wolf-and-ewe light).
How everything arrives: chāy on a sini, carried to guests, elders first. Sini-ye chāy ro becharkhun = pass the tea tray around.
From French ascenseur. Āsānsor kharābe = the elevator is broken (take the pelle). Bā āsānsor boro = take the lift.
